disclosingParty.description: Extract the full legal name of the disclosing party **without trailing punctuation or labels** (e.g., no trailing commas, no 'hereinafter').

receivingParty.description: Extract the full legal name of the receiving party **without trailing punctuation or labels**.

effectiveDate.description: Extract the effective date exactly as printed (e.g., '16 March 2016', 'March 16, 2016', or '16/03/2016').

mutualOrUnilateral.description: Classify NDA type: 'mutual' if both disclose; 'unilateral' if one discloses; 'unknown' if not determinable.

definitionOfConfidentialInfo.description: Return the **full definition clause** describing Confidential Information (no headings, no spillover to other sections).

exclusionsFromConfidentialInfo.description: Return the **full exclusions clause** (e.g., public domain, prior possession, independent development, authorized release).

permittedUse.description: Extract the clause describing how the Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information. Return the exact sentence or phrase that states the permitted use. If multiple sentences define permitted use, extract the one most clearly stating use limitations.

permittedDisclosures.description: Extract the statement in the NDA that describes when the Receiving Party is allowed to disclose the Confidential Information. Return only the clause that specifies any permitted disclosures (e.g., disclosures allowed to certain recipients or under certain contractual carve-outs). Do not include the separate legal-disclosure or 'required by law' clause unless it is explicitly part of the same sentence.

requiredDisclosureProtocol.description: Protocol when disclosure is legally required (e.g., prompt notice to the disclosing party and cooperation to limit scope).

returnOrDestructionObligations.description: Obligations to return/destroy Confidential Information upon request/termination, including any certification requirement.

confidentialityTermYears.description: Numeric duration (in years) the confidentiality obligations survive after termination/expiry (e.g., 5).

survivalClauses.description: Text of the survival clause covering confidentiality and any other continuing obligations.

confidentialitySurvivalTrigger.description: Event that starts survival clock (e.g., 'from termination' or 'from expiry').

intellectualPropertyOwnership.description: Extract any clause addressing ownership or control of Confidential Information or intellectual property, including statements denying or granting any rights, licenses, or usage privileges.

noLicenseGranted.description: Clause clarifying no license or other rights are granted by disclosure.

injunctiveRelief.description: Extract any clause that provides the Disclosing Party or either Party with rights to seek  injunctive, interim, equitable, or court‑ordered relief. This includes statements recognizing  irreparable harm, entitlement to injunctive relief, or any clause specifying that a Party  may seek temporary or interim measures in court.

liabilityLimitations.description: Limitation/exclusion of liability (e.g., no indirect, incidental, consequential damages).

assignmentClause.description: Restrictions on assigning the Agreement or rights (e.g., 'no assignment without written consent').

noticeProcedure.description: Notice delivery method(s), addresses, and deemed-receipt rules (if present).

amendmentClause.description: Requirement that amendments/modifications must be in writing and signed (if present).

terminationNoticeDays.description: Numeric notice period (in days) required to terminate/withdraw (e.g., 30).

publicityOrTrademarkRestriction.description: Restriction on using the other party's name, trademarks, or logos in publicity/marketing without prior written consent.

dataSecurityRequirements.description: Security requirements (e.g., encryption, access controls, breach notice); leave blank if not specified.

dataResidencyRequirements.description: Data localization/cross-border transfer requirements; leave blank if not specified.

personalDataProcessing.description: Does the NDA explicitly address personal data/privacy (e.g., processing, PII, privacy law carve-outs)? Choose yes/no/unspecified.

governingLaw.description: Governing law for the Agreement (e.g., 'Laws of India', 'State of California, USA').

arbitrationRulesOrStatute.description: Arbitration rules or statute (e.g., 'Arbitration & Conciliation Act, 1996', 'ICC Rules').

arbitratorAppointment.description: Appointment & composition of tribunal (e.g., 'sole arbitrator mutually appointed by the parties').

disputeResolutionMechanism.description: Narrative description of dispute resolution (e.g., escalation, mediation, arbitration steps).

jurisdiction.description: Court jurisdiction (if specified) separate from arbitration (e.g., for injunctive relief).

parties.description: Extract all parties and signers as a single string using this format: \   PARTY::<PartyName> | ROLE::<disclosing|receiving|mutual|unknown> | SIGNERS::<Signer1Name>^^<Signer1Title>^^<Signer1Place>^^<YYYY-MM-DD> ;; <Signer2Name>^^<Signer2Title>^^<Signer2Place>^^<YYYY-MM-DD> \   || PARTY::<PartyName> | ROLE::<...> | SIGNERS::<...>. \   Use '||' between parties, ';;' between multiple signers for a party, and '^^' between signer fields. Strip labels in the source like 'Name:', 'Designation:', 'Place:'. Strip trailing punctuation.

missingRequiredClauses.description: Comma-separated list of clauses expected by this schema but **absent** in the document (e.g., noticeProcedure, amendmentClause, dataSecurityRequirements, dataResidencyRequirements).

unknownClauses.description: Clauses found that do not map to any known field (for diagnostics).

extractedConflicts.description: Detected contradictions (e.g., multiple confidentiality durations, conflicting governing laws).

ndaSummary.description: Business-friendly summary of key NDA terms (1–3 sentences).

ndaRiskAssessment.description: Risk notes highlighting omissions (e.g., missing notices/amendments, lack of security/residency) or aggressive terms.
